signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Decision + Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.21 — BoJ is set to announce its monetary policy decision today (16 Jun), with market expectations for a rate hike as prices rise. This comes as the pair trades above the 160.00 intervention watch level, increasing the risk of BoJ action. The 292bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry differential (SOFR 3.65% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to favor USD, sustaining the bid despite JPY strength ahead of the decision. JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (long%=29.2%) indicates a crowded short, vulnerable to a squeeze if the BoJ delivers a hawkish surprise. The 25d RR at -1.20 shows moderate put skew, suggesting options market hedging against JPY strength. Watch for BoJ's forward guidance and any explicit comments on FX levels.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Decision + Negative CESI + Vol Premium
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7065 — The RBA is widely expected to keep rates on hold at 4.35% today (16 Jun), marking its first pause of 2026. This aligns with a moderate negative bias in the AUD CESI (net=-8 misses), indicating data is consistently disappointing expectations. AUD COT LF net long +38,092 (long%=68.8%) shows significant speculative long positioning, which is likely to unwind if the RBA signals a dovish pause or if future hike bets weaken further. The AUD/USD IV at 15.1% vs HV30 at 8.1% (VRP +7.0%) indicates elevated options market hedging demand, pricing in higher volatility around the RBA decision. Watch for RBA's statement language on future policy path and inflation outlook.

signals.title: USD/CAD — COT vs Retail Divergence + Oil Price Impact
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3998 — Retail sentiment shows an extreme 92% short position, indicating strong contrarian reversal risk. This diverges from institutional positioning, where CAD COT LF net short −60,671 (long%=24.7%) suggests a crowded short in CAD futures. The recent news of crude oil prices sinking on the deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz puts downward pressure on WTI, which typically correlates negatively with USD/CAD. However, the USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has broken to -0.278 vs 252d norm=+0.007 (z=-1.74σ), suggesting the usual inverse relationship is currently weaker. The 140bp OIS carry differential (SOFR 3.65% vs CORRA 2.25%) favors USD. Watch for WTI oil price stability and any shift in retail positioning to confirm a reversal.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Options Skew + Vol Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.159 — The 25d RR at -0.34 indicates a mild put skew, signaling options market participants are paying for downside protection for EUR. This is despite the EUR/USD IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.2%, resulting in a +2.9% VRP, which suggests elevated hedging demand over realized volatility. EUR COT LF net short −49,290 (long%=37.4%) shows a significant speculative short position. The 172bp OIS carry differential (SOFR 3.65% vs €STR 1.93%) favors USD, structurally limiting EUR/USD upside. Watch for a sustained break above 1.1600 to challenge the options market's downside bias.
